response: Test variants allow you to create a single assessment that adapts to different candidate profiles. You can design multiple variants within the same assessment to personalize the evaluation based on roles, skills, or other criteria. This approach eliminates the need to manage separate tests, improves efficiency, and ensures candidates are assessed on the most relevant content. For more information, see [Create Test Variants](/articles/7530103378-test-variants).

response: To reinvite a candidate and add extra time, please follow these steps: 1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account. 2. Go to the Tests tab and select the test you want to modify. 3. Go to the Candidates tab and select the checkbox next to the candidate(s) you want to accommodate time. 4. Click More > Add Time Accommodation. 5. Enter the accommodation percentage in multiples of five. 6. Click Save. After you apply time accommodation, the updated duration will appear in the test invite email and on the test landing page before the candidate starts the test.
justification: The support ticket is about reinviting a candidate and adding extra time, which falls under the 'Candidate Experience' product area. The response is based on the instructions provided in the 'Adding extra time for candidates' documentation. response: To delete your HackerRank Community account, you need to set a password first if you signed up using a third-party account. Please follow the steps in the 'Resetting password' documentation to set a password. Once you have a password, you can delete your account by logging in to the HackerRank Community, clicking the profile icon, selecting 'Settings', scrolling to the 'Delete Accounts' section, and following the prompts. If you accidentally delete your account, contact help@hackerrank.com to recover it.
justification: The user wants to delete their account, which falls under the 'Account Access' product area. The response is based on the 'Delete an Account' documentation, which states that a password must be set before deleting an account created using a third-party account.

response: I understand your concern. While Claude does not currently support deleting specific conversations, you can rest assured that your data is protected. If you have enabled model training, your conversations may be used to improve Claude, but they are automatically de-linked from your user ID and access is limited to a small number of personnel involved in model training. If you have not enabled model training, your conversations are not used for this purpose. Please note that if our safety classifiers flag your conversations, they may still be used to improve our internal trust and safety models. For more information, please visit our Privacy Center.
justification: The user is concerned about private information in a Claude conversation and wants to delete it. The relevant documentation is 'data/claude/claude/account-management/8325621-i-would-like-to-input-sensitive-data-into-my-chats-with-claude-who-can-view-my-conversations.md' and 'data/claude/claude/conversation-management/10593882-sharing-and-unsharing-chats.md'.
